title: President Trump's Threats to Iran: What Experts Say
summary: President Donald Trump said he might destroy bridges and power plants in Iran. Experts in military law think this could be a war crime. A war crime is when actions in war hurt civilians too much or are not fair. The experts say that if the power plants are not military targets, or if many civilians get hurt, it could break international law. Some people from the United Nations and Democrats in Congress agree. The president often talks big but does not always do what he says. He gave Iran a deadline to open a waterway called the Strait of Hormuz. A U.N. spokesman warned that attacking such things is not allowed under the rules of war.

keywords.explanation: an action in war that hurts civilians too much or is unfair
rules that countries agree to follow, especially in war
people who are not soldiers and should be protected in war
things like enemy bases that are okay to attack in war
a group of countries that work together for peace and rules

questions.question: What did President Trump threaten to destroy in Iran?
What do experts say this threat could be?
Who might get hurt if these attacks happen?
What is the name of the waterway Iran was told to open?
Which group warned that attacking infrastructure is not allowed?
What do experts check to see if an attack is fair?
options: If it is proportional and minimizes civilian harm
If it is quick and cheap
questions.correct_answer: If it is proportional and minimizes civilian harm

background_read: War crimes are serious actions that break rules in war, like hurting civilians. International law helps countries agree on how to act during conflicts. The United Nations is an organization that tries to keep peace and enforce these rules. Civilians are people who are not fighting and should be kept safe. Military targets are things like enemy weapons or bases that are okay to attack. When leaders make threats, experts check if they follow these rules to protect people.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and experts in military law
WHAT: Threats to destroy Iran's infrastructure, which could be a war crime
WHY: To pressure Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and because experts say it might break international law

perspectives.description: They believe the threats could be war crimes if they harm civilians or are not proportional.
